reasoning: With only 52 days remaining in 2025 until the resolution date (December 31) and no ongoing outbreaks or WHO declarations of a new coronavirus as of the knowledge cutoff (November 9, 2025), a new pandemic declaration is highly improbable. The timeline from the spillover of a novel pathogen to global spread and a formal WHO pandemic declaration typically takes several months. Since there are absolutely no precursor events or Public Health Emergencies of International Concern (PHEIC) for a new coronavirus, the likelihood is effectively zero.
